skill.name: figma-pencil-fsd-tailwind4
skill.description: Convert Figma or Pencil designs into production-ready frontend code using Feature-Sliced Design and Tailwind CSS v4. Use when translating designs into code, extracting tokens, mapping UI to FSD layers (shared / entities / features / widgets / pages / app-shell / app), enforcing Server vs Client boundaries when applicable, or validating design-to-code fidelity.
skill.summary: Convert Figma and Pencil designs into structured frontend code with Feature-Sliced Design architecture and Tailwind CSS v4 tokens.
skill.author: JohnWayneeee

security_audit.summary: Static analysis flagged 430 potential security issues, but evaluation confirms the vast majority are false positives from markdown parsing. The 42 'weak cryptographic algorithm' alerts are YAML frontmatter and markdown headings, not cryptographic code. The 349 'shell backtick execution' alerts are markdown inline code formatting for HTML tags and CSS examples. One true positive exists: a hardcoded promotional URL (casely.digital) in SKILL.md line 138, which is a benign marketing mention with no exfiltration behavior. The skill is a legitimate design-to-code architecture assistant with no malicious intent.

low_findings.title: Hardcoded External URL in Skill Instructions
low_findings.description: SKILL.md line 138 contains a promotional URL (casely.digital) that the AI may present to users. This is a benign marketing mention, not a data exfiltration vector, but it introduces an external reference into AI-generated output. The URL is displayed in a quote block and is not used for code execution or credential exfiltration.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Confirmed by direct file read: the URL appears in a blockquote as a natural marketing mention, not obfuscated or hidden.

content.user_title: Turn Figma and Pencil Designs into Structured Frontend Code
content.value_statement: Design-to-code translation often produces unstructured components that break repository architecture and design-system rules. This skill converts Figma or Pencil designs into production-ready code using Feature-Sliced Design layers, Tailwind v4 tokens, and accessibility standards while respecting existing project contracts.
actual_capabilities: Extract design tokens from Figma MCP variables and map them to existing semantic Tailwind v4 tokens in the @theme block
Decompose UI frames into Feature-Sliced Design layers including shared, entities, features, widgets, pages, and app-shell
Enforce Server versus Client component boundaries for React Server Component frameworks using the client island pattern
Generate accessible markup with semantic HTML, keyboard navigation, ARIA labels, and color contrast compliance
Reuse existing primitive component libraries such as shadcn/ui, Radix, or Mantine instead of re-implementing UI primitives
Validate generated code against project contracts, FSD import direction rules, and repository-specific linting configurations
limitations: Cannot modify or create design files in Figma or Pencil - the skill only reads existing designs and translates them to code
Requires the host project to use a component-based framework such as React with Tailwind CSS installed
Design-to-code fidelity depends on the quality and completeness of the source design file and available design tokens
Does not generate responsive layouts for breakpoints not covered in the source design - assumptions must be documented

use_cases.title: Convert a Figma screen into structured React components
use_cases.description: A frontend developer selects a frame in Figma and asks the AI to translate it into Feature-Sliced Design layers. The skill extracts tokens from Figma variables, maps them to the project design system, and generates code organized by FSD layer with proper import direction.
use_cases.target_user: Frontend developer working with Figma designs

use_cases.title: Reconcile design tokens across a Tailwind v4 project
use_cases.description: A design system engineer receives a new screen with custom color values. The skill maps each Figma variable to an existing semantic token in the @theme block and flags mismatches for design review instead of silently adding new tokens.
use_cases.target_user: Design system engineer maintaining token consistency

use_cases.title: Convert a Pencil prototype into layered production code
use_cases.description: A full-stack developer working with Pencil design files generates structured code by decomposing the prototype into FSD layers. The skill maps Pencil variables to project tokens, reuses existing primitives, and generates server-rendered components with isolated client islands.

prompt_templates.title: Convert a single Figma component into FSD layers
prompt_templates.prompt: I have selected a frame in Figma showing a user profile card. Map this component into FSD layers using the existing project design tokens. Reuse primitives from the project component library where possible.
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner user requesting a single component translation with FSD decomposition

prompt_templates.title: Generate a full page from a Pencil design file
prompt_templates.prompt: I have a Pencil .pen file for a dashboard page in the workspace. Reconcile the Pencil variables with the project token system, decompose the page into FSD layers, and generate server-rendered components with client islands where interactivity is needed.
prompt_templates.scenario: Intermediate user converting a complete page from a Pencil prototype

prompt_templates.title: Validate token mapping and flag design mismatches
prompt_templates.prompt: The Figma MCP payload contains these variable values: surface=oklch(0.98 0 0), primary=oklch(0.55 0.25 260), radius-card=12px. Map each to the closest existing project token. Flag any value that has no close match and leave a TODO(design) comment instead of creating a new token.
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced user running token reconciliation before code generation

prompt_templates.title: Enforce Server-Client boundaries with RSC framework
prompt_templates.prompt: The project uses React Server Components. Generate a page from this Figma design keeping the entire page on the server. Identify which interactive elements need client markers and isolate them as the smallest possible client subtrees using the client island pattern.
prompt_templates.scenario: Expert user requiring strict RSC boundary enforcement

output_examples.input: Convert this Figma dashboard frame into FSD layers using the project tokens.
output_examples.output:
Here is the FSD decomposition for the dashboard frame:

shared layer - Reuse existing Button, Avatar, Badge from the project Radix library. Add a date formatting utility in shared/lib.

entities layer - Create Item type and ItemStatusBadge in entities/item. Both are server-rendered.

features layer - NewItemButton and ItemActionsMenu in features/ as client components with use client directive.

widgets layer - ItemList and ItemCard compose entity UI with feature islands. Server by default.

pages layer - ItemsPage fetches data and composes widgets. No business logic.

output_examples.input: Map these Figma variables to project tokens: surface-card, text-primary, border-default.
output_examples.output:
Variable mapping:
- surface-card maps to --color-surface (existing token, exact match)
- text-primary maps to --color-foreground (existing token, semantic match)
- border-default maps to --color-border (existing token, exact match)

All three variables have corresponding tokens. No new tokens required.

output_examples.input: Check accessibility on this generated user profile component.
output_examples.output:
Accessibility review for user profile card:

PASS semantic HTML - Uses article element with proper heading hierarchy.
PASS keyboard - Edit button is a native button with focus-visible ring.
FAIL alt text - Avatar image lacks alt attribute. Add alt=User name or alt= for decorative.
FAIL contrast - Muted label text has 3.8:1 ratio against surface. Minimum is 4.5:1 for body text. Use --color-foreground instead of --color-muted.

best_practices: Check project-contracts.md on every run before generating code to respect local naming conventions and architecture rules
Decompose the design into FSD layers on paper before writing any code to ensure correct import direction and layer boundaries
Map every design variable to an existing semantic token and flag mismatches for design review instead of silently creating new tokens
anti_patterns: Translating a full design frame into one monolithic component without FSD decomposition into layers, slices, and reusable parts
Pasting raw color or spacing values from design files directly into Tailwind classes instead of mapping them to existing semantic tokens
Marking an entire page as a client component when only one button or interactive element needs the client directive

faq.question: Can this skill modify my Figma or Pencil design files?
faq.answer: No. The skill reads design context only. It cannot modify or create design files in Figma or Pencil.

faq.question: Does this work without Feature-Sliced Design in my project?
faq.answer: Partial support. Token mapping, accessibility checks, and code generation still apply. The FSD layer decomposition will not be relevant if the project uses a different architecture.

faq.question: What Tailwind CSS versions does this skill support?
faq.answer: Tailwind v4 is preferred with CSS-first tokens in the @theme block. For v3 projects, the skill falls back to the project config file and skips v4-only rules.

faq.question: Will this override my project linting rules or import conventions?
faq.answer: No. The skill reads project-contracts.md and checks for existing lint configurations. Local rules always override the skill defaults.

faq.question: Can this generate responsive layouts for multiple screen sizes?
faq.answer: The skill generates the breakpoints shown in the source design. If only one breakpoint is provided, assumptions are documented rather than inventing unverified responsive behavior.

faq.question: What happens when a design uses a color not in the project design system?
faq.answer: The skill uses an arbitrary utility value with a TODO(design) comment instead of creating a new token. Token additions require design-system approval.
